notes: Batch of 151 identical 5-vertex closed LWPOLYLINEs on the INSUL layer of the 평면도(기본형) floor-plan xref; insulation-fill pattern, not wall-boundary geometry.

rationale.evidence: All 151 entities are LWPOLYLINE with 5 vertices (closed quads) on a single layer, X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL, whose $0$INSUL suffix marks the insulation-fill layer of the '평면도(기본형)' floor-plan xref. Handles run in one tight contiguous block (3C70-3C8D and onward), consistent with a single batch-generated hatch/tick pattern rather than a hand-placed multi-purpose symbol. No LINE, INSERT, or WIPEOUT entities appear, so there is no explicit wall-boundary or fixture geometry in this definition.
rationale.rule: A block made entirely of many identical small closed polylines on a layer name containing 'INSUL' should be classified as a floor-plan insulation-fill fragment (평면 부분도), not as a discrete symbol, dimension cache, or furniture, and its wall_likelihood should be moderate rather than high because it depicts fill material rather than the wall boundary line itself.
rationale.counterfactual: If LINE entities forming a long rectilinear pair (the two edges of a wall) were present, or if the layer read 'WALL' instead of 'INSUL', wall_likelihood would rise sharply and specific handles would be flagged in wall_line_handles.
rationale.uncertainty: Without vertex coordinates (only 'vertices=5' is given, no bbox), the actual size and placement of each polyline relative to a wall cavity cannot be confirmed, so whether this fill sits inside an interior or exterior wall assembly is undeterminable from this projection alone.

notes: Batch of 16 identical 5-vertex closed LWPOLYLINEs on the INSUL layer of the 평면도(기본형) floor-plan xref; insulation-fill pattern, not wall-boundary geometry.

rationale.evidence: All 16 entities are LWPOLYLINE with 5 vertices (closed quads) on a single layer, X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL, whose $0$INSUL suffix marks the insulation-fill layer of the '평면도(기본형)' floor-plan xref. Handles run in one tight contiguous block (4013-4022), consistent with a single batch-generated hatch/tick pattern rather than a hand-placed multi-purpose symbol. No LINE, INSERT, or WIPEOUT entities appear, so there is no explicit wall-boundary or fixture geometry in this definition.
rationale.rule: A block made entirely of many identical small closed polylines on a layer name containing 'INSUL' should be classified as a floor-plan insulation-fill fragment (평면 부분도), not as a discrete symbol, dimension cache, or furniture, and its wall_likelihood should be moderate rather than high because it depicts fill material rather than the wall boundary line itself.
rationale.counterfactual: If LINE entities forming a long rectilinear pair (the two edges of a wall) were present, or if the layer read 'WALL' instead of 'INSUL', wall_likelihood would rise sharply and specific handles would be flagged in wall_line_handles.
rationale.uncertainty: Without vertex coordinates (only 'vertices=5' is given, no bbox), the actual size and placement of each polyline relative to a wall cavity cannot be confirmed, so whether this fill sits inside an interior or exterior wall assembly is undeterminable from this projection alone.
